jobDescription: Who are Meta Pack? We are a tech company that works with a lot of the world’s biggest ecommerce players to integrate them with over 450 carriers around the world to make delivery easy. We are a multi-tenant Saa S platform. We give them the platform to help consumers decide their delivery preference and track the parcel’s progress whilst also providing the retailer with intelligent smart decisions about how to send the parcel – all underpinned with lots of data. We work with well-known global retailers and major brands such as ASOS, Adidas, Burberry, John Lewis, Boohoo, e Bay, and Zalando. In fact we work with so many retailers and carriers it’s highly likely that you’ve interacted with us at some point when ordering goods online! In August 2018, we were acquired by Fortune 100’s 2nd fastest growing company, stamps.com. We have super ambitious and exciting plans all centred around our tech. Meta Pack will play a role in shipping around 600 million parcels in 2018 and with the wider stamps.com family the number rises to 2.5bn parcels. Meta Pack has been growing at 40% year on year over the last 5 years and continues to grow at a rapid rate. Why would I want to be a Product Manager at Meta Pack? At Meta Pack, a Product Manager has the unique role of shaping the future of ecommerce delivery for the world’s largest retailers and their consumers.  The role provides opportunities to work with many of the world’s largest brands and retailers (e.g. Adidas, John Lewis, ASOS, Zalando etc.) with a world class technology team and with volumes and transactions and date not found anywhere else in the ecommerce delivery space. What would I be doing? Producing, maintaining and advocating the business product vision and roadmap to represent the company's goals and support the continued growth of the Meta Pack business You want to understand " What", " Why" and “ When” more than " How" of Meta Pack’s retail products Creating vision and strategy for your product area and are able to inspire and influence stakeholders and customers. Providing clear analysis on customers, global markets, opportunities, growth profiles, segmentation and competitor analysis  Owning the design and delivery of the product roadmap – you will prioritise ruthlessly and will know when to trade-off good ideas. Making sure that the product output is to a consistent high standard across all product outputs such as sales and marketing collateral and documentation, a clear product rate card and contract product description as well as a consistent and relevant user experience ( UX) of both the user interfaces and APIs Managing the product life-cycle, from inception to end-of-life Responsible for the Product Development Lifecycle including management of existing products and NPD, including Product Innovation Develop take to market strategies working with key commercial (including Sales, Marketing & Partners) and technical stakeholders Commercially responsible for your products revenue and its pricing strategy Responsible for KPI/ OKR describing business outcomes including expected revenue Responsible for ensuring that regular product management reporting, sales enablement and product collateral are completed Engaging with Customers, PMO, Project Owners and Solutions Architects to understand and plan customer commitments What key skills and experience do I need? Experience in the commerciality and marketing of a B2 B software product, ensuring the products continual success in its market A keen interest in the omnichannel delivery domain and the technologies that impact it The ability to influence without authority Strong track record in delivering agreed outputs and outcomes A strong appreciation of user experience ( UX) from multiple points of view and the ability to translate this into product design An appreciation of product practises, processes and tools Excellent interpersonal and communication skills Good problem identification and solving skills Strong organisational and analytical skills Ability to work in fast-paced, time-compressed and dynamic environment Bachelor’s degree or equivalent Translating requirements into actionable, profitable plans Working across international teams and time zones It would be great if you also could bring Any logistics, supply chain, retail or ecommerce software experience An appreciation of marketing and product marketing What are the perks?25 days holiday, 10% bonus (paid quarterly), pension, enhanced maternity and paternity leave, group life insurance scheme, private medical healthcare Discounted gym membership, cycle to work scheme, interest free season ticket loan Breakfast, dinner, fresh fruit, snacks and drinks Dynamic, open culture with lots of social activities
